items.title: Residents blast 50% tax cut for Oracle's $43B Saline data center
items.dek: Two locals say the 12-year abatement would leave neighbors facing higher power bills and pollution while the company avoids paying its share.
items.judge_note: Concrete new development: residents testifying against a specific $43B tax abatement proposal in Saline, a clear next step beyond the earlier delay story.

items.title: Adrian Hits Pause on New Data Centers to Safeguard Water, Power and Neighborhoods
items.dek: City enacts year-long moratorium on proposals, giving officials time to craft rules before big projects strain resources.
items.judge_note: Adrian enacts a year-long moratorium—a definitive vote/decision in a new municipality, directly parallel to the Houghton action already live.

items.title: Girard Township Tightens Data Center Zoning Rules as Coldwater Prepares Its Own Safeguards
items.dek: New limits aim to curb impacts on farmland and infrastructure while neighboring city drafts matching protections.
items.judge_note: Girard Township adopts new zoning rules (beyond the earlier single-parcel confinement) and Coldwater is drafting its own—two concrete municipal actions.

items.title: House Democrats File Data Center Rules on Power Costs, Water Use and Noise
items.dek: Bills would require developers to cover full electricity expenses, sign community agreements and face water limits as projects advance across Michigan.
items.judge_note: House Democrats file specific bills with concrete provisions—a legislative filing is a tangible development, though national in scope.

items.title: Rural Township Postpones Oracle Data Center Tax Break Vote Amid Resident Backlash
items.dek: Washtenaw County officials delay decision on 12-year abatement for $43 billion project near Ann Arbor after community opposition highlights concerns over infrastructure strain and local impacts.
items.judge_note: A specific vote postponement on a major tax abatement is a concrete decision/action, distinct from the earlier killed story about Oracle's initial tax request.

items.title: Tyrone Township Residents Demand Answers on Secret Data Center Talks with Officials
items.dek: A local resident grilled township leaders at a recent meeting over possible nondisclosure agreements signed with developers, highlighting ongoing transparency worries even as a temporary moratorium remains in place.
items.judge_note: Incremental transparency angle in Tyrone Township; real resident confrontation but lacks a specific decision or filing.

items.title: OpenAI-Oracle Data Center to Draw 1.4 Gigawatts in Saline Township
items.dek: The 1-gigawatt AI campus on 250 acres will create 450 onsite jobs and receive sales-tax relief plus a 12-year local abatement; DTE is separately negotiating another 3 gigawatts of data-center load in Michigan.
items.judge_note: Major new project confirmation: 1.4 GW OpenAI-Oracle campus with specific job numbers, acreage, and tax abatement details — concrete development.

items.title: Saline Township continues data center meetings on groundwater and site monitoring
items.dek: After a consent judgment allowing the project, township leaders schedule repeated advisory sessions to review water well data and related plans.
items.judge_note: Incremental follow-up on Saline with scheduled advisory sessions and monitoring—real next step after the consent judgment, not a duplicate of the tax break stor
